column1: # Port manifest — the authoritative work list for making mcfish a bit-exact 1:1
# THE PORT PATH IS zfish -> mcfish, NOT Stockfish -> mcfish.
# ../zfish is a complete, bit-exact Zig port of Stockfish. It has already done the
# hard structural work that C cannot express directly: templates, classes, RAII,
# operator overloading, and exceptions are all gone, and the engine is decomposed
# into small single-responsibility modules. Translating that Zig into C23 is a far
# smaller job than translating Stockfish's C++, and it starts from code that is
# ../Stockfish stays the GOLDEN: it defines correct behaviour, and the differential
# gate compares against a pristine upstream build, never against zfish. Where zfish
# and Stockfish disagree, Stockfish wins and the zfish divergence is a bug report.
#     PORTED   behaviour matches upstream, proven by a gate
#              this module's behaviour lives in a LARGER mcfish file, named in the
#              owner column, rather than in a file of its own. The row stays so the
#              source module still maps somewhere; the owner is what is gated.
#     PARTIAL  compiles and runs, but is NOT upstream-faithful yet
#     TODO     not started; the mcfish path is reserved, not written
#     SKIP     deliberately not ported; reason in the trailing comment
#   risk ∈ {LOW, MED, HIGH} — blast radius on the bench signature.
# Every TODO row is REQUIRED. NNUE, Syzygy and NUMA are not optional extras; the
# engine is not a Stockfish clone without them. See docs/PORTING.md for the order.
